chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Imagine you happen across a short movie script that describes a scene between a person and their AI assistant.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: The script has what the person asks the AI, but the AI's response has been torn off.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Suppose you also have this powerful magical machine that can take any text and provide a sensible prediction of what word comes next.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: You could then finish the script by feeding in what you have to the machine, seeing what it would predict to start the AI's answer, and then repeating this over and over with a growing script completing the dialogue.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: When you interact with a chatbot, this is exactly what's happening.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: A large language model is a sophisticated mathematical function that predicts what word comes next for any piece of text.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Instead of predicting one word with certainty, though, what it does is assign a probability to all possible next words.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: To build a chatbot, what you do is lay out some text that describes an interaction between a user and a hypothetical AI assistant.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: You add on whatever the user types in as the first part of that interaction,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: and then you have the model repeatedly predict the next word that such a hypothetical AI assistant would say in response, and that's what's presented to the user.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: In doing this, the output tends to look a lot more natural if you allow it to select less likely words along the way at random.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: So what this means is even though the model itself is deterministic, a given prompt typically gives a different answer each time it's run.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Models learn how to make these predictions by processing an enormous amount of text, typically pulled from the internet.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: For a standard human to read the amount of text that was used to train GPT-3, for example, if they read non-stop 24-7, it would take over 2600 years.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Larger models since then train on much, much more.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: You can think of training a little bit like tuning the dials on a big machine.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: The way that a language model behaves is entirely determined by these many different continuous values, usually called parameters or weights.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Changing those parameters will change the probabilities that the model gives for the next word on a given input.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: What puts the large in large language model is how they can have hundreds of billions of these parameters.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: No human ever deliberately sets those parameters.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Instead, they begin at random, meaning the model just outputs gibberish, but they are repeatedly refined based on many example pieces of text.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: One of these training examples could be just a handful of words, or it could be thousands,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: but in either case, the way this works is to pass in all but the last word from that example into the model,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: and compare the prediction that it makes with the true last word from the example.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: An algorithm called backpropagation is used to tweak all of the parameters in such a way that it makes the model a little more likely to choose the true last word,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: and a little less likely to choose all the others.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: When you do this for many, many trillions of examples, not only does the model start to give more accurate predictions on the training data,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: but it also starts to make more reasonable predictions on text that it's never seen before.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Given the huge number of parameters and the enormous amount of training data,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: the scale of computation involved in training a large language model is mind-boggling.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: To illustrate, imagine that you could perform one billion additions and multiplications every single second.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: How long do you think that it would take for you to do all of the operations involved in training the largest language models?

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Do you think it would take a year? Maybe something like 10,000 years?

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: The answer is actually much more than that. It's well over 100 million years.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: This is only part of the story, though. This whole process is called pre-training.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: The goal of auto-completing a random passage of text from the internet is very different from the goal of being a good AI assistant.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: To address this, chatbots undergo another type of training, just as important, called reinforcement learning with human feedback.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Workers flag unhelpful or problematic predictions, and their corrections further change the model's parameters,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: making them more likely to give predictions that users prefer.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Looking back at the pre-training, though, this staggering amount of computation is only made possible by using special computer chips

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: that are optimized for running many, many operations in parallel, known as GPUs.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: However, not all language models can be easily parallelized.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Prior to 2017, most language models would process text one word at a time.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: But then, a team of researchers at Google introduced a new model known as the Transformer.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Transformers don't read text from the start to the finish. They soak it all in at once, in parallel.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: The very first step inside a Transformer, and most other language models for that matter,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: is to associate each word with a long list of numbers.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: The reason for this is that the training process only works with continuous values,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: so you have to somehow encode language using numbers,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: and each of these lists of numbers may somehow encode the meaning of the corresponding word.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: What makes Transformers unique is their reliance on a special operation known as attention.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: This operation gives all of these lists of numbers a chance to talk to one another,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: and refine the meanings that they encode based on the context around, all done in parallel.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: For example, the numbers encoding the word bank might be changed based on the context surrounding it,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: to somehow encode the more specific notion of a riverbank.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Transformers typically also include a second type of operation, known as a feed-forward neural network,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: and this gives the model extra capacity to store more patterns about language learned during training.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: All of this data repeatedly flows through many different iterations of these two fundamental operations,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: and as it does so, the hope is that each list of numbers is enriched to encode whatever information might be needed

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: to make an accurate prediction of what word follows in the passage.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: At the end, one final function is performed on the last vector in this sequence,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: which now has had a chance to be influenced by all the other context from the input text,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: as well as everything the model learned during training,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: to produce a prediction of the next word.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Again, the model's prediction looks like a probability for every possible next word.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Although researchers design the framework for how each of these steps work,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: it's important to understand that the specific behavior is an emergent phenomenon

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: based on how those hundreds of billions of parameters are tuned during training.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: This makes it incredibly challenging to determine why the model makes the exact predictions that it does.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: What you can see is that when you use large language model predictions to auto-complete a prompt,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: the words that it generates are uncannily fluent, fascinating, and even useful.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: If you're a new viewer and you're curious about more details on how transformers and attention work,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: boy do I have some material for you!

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: One option is to jump into a series I made about deep learning,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: where we visualize and motivate the details of attention and all the other steps in a transformer.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: But also, on my second channel I just posted a talk that I gave a couple months ago about this topic

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: for the company TNG in Munich.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Sometimes I actually prefer the content that I make as a casual talk rather than a produced video,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: but I leave it up to you which one of these feels like the better follow-on.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: This all works creative는데요 like this in the world.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Internet music it takes so many sequences zijn.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: If you download our content, Monique f Durham Jeter had a forestiertel 7ulf of a 7 fox team.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Don't forget to demand for your wish,...

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: 我生 job suivatiens.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: Give yourself a deep

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: יד six days,

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: please check out what you do.

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: I'm happy to follow our listeners

chunks.title: Large Language Models explained briefly
chunks.text: at the end or dej poser seus sc рублей.
